name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram is a money transfer service offering cash sending and receiving at retail locations like CVS pharmacies across the US, with extended hours and same-day processing.
description_long:
MoneyGram operates as a global money transfer network, enabling customers to send and receive funds domestically and internationally through a network of retail agent locations. The company has partnered with major pharmacy and convenience store chains, including CVS, to provide accessible money transfer services in communities nationwide. Their service model focuses on accessibility through extended retail hours and a simple process requiring only a legal form of identification.

MoneyGram's core offering includes domestic money sending and receiving, with services available both online and through physical locations. At their CVS partner locations, customers can conduct cash-related transactions during operating hours (8 AM to 11 PM daily at the Cleveland location referenced). The company emphasizes ease of use, allowing customers to manage transfers through their website or in-person at neighborhood retail partners.

MoneyGram distinguishes itself through its extensive retail footprint and partnership strategy. By operating through existing pharmacy chains and convenience stores rather than standalone locations, they provide convenient access in high-traffic areas. Their extended operating hours (open until 11 PM daily at this location) serve customers who need services outside traditional banking hours. The online management component adds flexibility for tech-savvy users.

MoneyGram is a straightforward money transfer service best suited for customers needing quick access to cash services at convenient retail locations. The main limitations are that this profile represents a single partner location, services are limited to money transfers and cash services, and customers must provide valid ID for all transactions. The service does not offer credit products, loans, or financial management tools beyond basic money movement.
pros: Extended operating hours until 11 PM daily, including weekends, providing access outside standard banking hours
Convenient retail locations at CVS pharmacies in neighborhood areas across the US
Services available both online and in-person for flexibility in how customers manage transfers
Simple process requiring only a legal form of ID
No minimum transaction amount specified, allowing flexibility for small transfers
National network with multiple locations searchable by address, city, state, or ZIP code
Dedicated customer service phone line (800) 746-7287 for location-specific support
cons: Limited to money transfer and cash services only—no credit, lending, or broader financial products
Fees not disclosed on website; customers must contact location or call for pricing information
Dependent on partner retail locations' availability; service quality may vary by CVS franchise
best_for: Individuals needing after-hours money transfer access outside traditional banking windows
Customers who prefer neighborhood retail locations over dedicated financial service storefronts
People sending domestic money transfers to family or for bill payment purposes
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for consumers who need convenient, after-hours access to domestic money transfer services through retail pharmacy locations rather than dedicated financial centers. The primary caveat is that this is a transactional service provider focused solely on money movement—it offers no credit products, lending, or financial management tools, and all fees and specific service offerings must be confirmed directly with the location.
services: In-person cash transactions at retail locations
Money order services (inferred from check-cashing category)
Wire transfer services (inferred from check-cashing category)
Bill payment services (inferred from check-cashing category)
